task: Browse the latest negative reviews from players with over 100 hours of playtime for the game that won the 2023 VR Game of the Year Award.
final_result_response:
Here's a comprehensive summary of the latest negative reviews from veteran players of **Labyrinthine**, the 2023 Steam VR Game of the Year Award winner:

---

**🎮 Game: Labyrinthine** (by Valko Game Studios)
**🏆 Award: 2023 Steam VR Game of the Year**
**Filters applied: Negative reviews | Playtime: Over 99 hours | Sorted by: Recent**

---

### Latest Negative Reviews from 100+ Hour Players:

**1. 灰 — 441.7 hrs | August 14, 2025**
Repeatedly lost cosmetics from seasonal events (Easter, summer, Halloween). Character level reset from 400+ to 284 and all cosmetics reverted. Friends' items remained intact while theirs vanished.

**2. xlazyfox — 252.6 hrs | July 23, 2025**
Enjoyed the story mode but everything after was a tedious grind. After ~250 hours, still hasn't earned half the cosmetics. Wants better drop rates for rare items.

**3. Gramdaddy69 — 130.4 hrs | July 18, 2025**
Angry about Xbox-exclusive cosmetics being released after Steam players funded the game through early access. Views it as a loyalty/betrayal issue.

**4. Onion — 391.6 hrs | January 13, 2025**
Negative experiences with Discord moderators (lying, favoritism). Ongoing bugs and lag issues. Sarcastically rates "10/10" while not recommending.

**5. Rohan — 282.5 hrs | May 15, 2024**
Waited from open beta to full release. Glitches persist (map holes, AI issues). Later story chapters declined in quality and felt repetitive.

**6. Maxwell Zentolight — 143.5 hrs | December 19, 2023**
After 100%-ing all achievements, criticizes artificially padded replayability. Ticket/cosmetic shop grind is "abysmal." Requires 150-200 hardcore cases for rare items.

**7. Akkallia — 255.2 hrs | August 18, 2023**
Enjoyed initially but updates didn't fix monster behavior. Cosmetics full of duplicates. Achievement counters were broken.

**8. Carnage — 246.1 hrs | August 8, 2023**
Quotes devs complaining about negative reviews hurting store visibility and criticizes them for guilt-tripping Discord members into posting positive reviews.

### Common Themes:
- 🔄 **Cosmetic grind** is extremely tedious and unrewarding
- 🐛 **Persistent bugs** from early access through full release
- 💀 **Vanishing cosmetics** frustrate dedicated players
- 🎮 **Xbox-exclusive content** angered loyal Steam supporters
- 📢 **Developer communication** and community management drew criticism
- ⏱️ **Achievement padding** artificially extends playtime

I can see the 2023 Steam Awards page. Let me scroll down to find the VR Game of the Year category.

I can see the 2023 VR Game of the Year Award winner is **Labyrinthine**. Now let me click on the game image to go to its store page.

I'm on the Labyrinthine store page. Now I need to scroll down to find the reviews section where I can filter for negative reviews from players with over 100 hours of playtime. Let me scroll down to the reviews section.
I can see the VR Game of the Year Award badge on the page confirming this is the right game. I need to scroll down further to reach the reviews section.

I can see the reviews section. I need to scroll up a bit to find the review filters where I can set it to show negative reviews and filter by playtime. Let me scroll up slightly to see the filter options.

1. First, change "Review Type" to "Negative"
2. Then, change "Playtime" to filter for over 100 hours

Let me click on "Review Type" first.
I can see the Review Type dropdown with options: All (17,082), Positive (14,455), and Negative (2,627). Let me click on "Negative".

I've selected "Negative" reviews. Now I can see negative reviews are showing. Next, I need to filter by Playtime to show only reviews from players with over 100 hours. Let me click on the "Playtime" filter.

I can see the Playtime filter dropdown with options:
- No Minimum (selected)
- Over 1 hour
- Over 10 hours
- And a slider for minimum to maximum

I don't see a direct "Over 100 hours" option, but there's a slider. Let me first check "Over 10 hours" and then use the slider to set it to 100 hours. Let me click on "Over 10 hours" first, then adjust the minimum slider.

I've selected "Over 10 hours" but I need "Over 100 hours". I can see there's a slider with "10 hour(s) to No maximum". I need to drag the left slider to the right to increase the minimum to 100 hours. Let me try to use the slider to set minimum to 100 hours. The left slider handle appears to be at around x=520, y=460. I need to drag it to the right.

Let me try dragging the left slider handle to the right to increase the minimum playtime.
I can see the slider is now at "39 hour(s) to No maximum". I need to drag it further to get to 100 hours. Let me continue dragging the left slider to the right.
Now it shows "65 hour(s) to No maximum". I need to drag it a bit more to reach 100 hours. Let me continue.
Now it shows "78 hour(s) to No maximum". I need to drag it a bit more to reach 100 hours. Let me continue dragging.
Now at "85 hour(s)". I need to get to 100. Let me drag a bit more.
Now at 89 hours. Let me drag a tiny bit more to reach 100.
Now at 92 hours. Let me try a slightly larger drag to get to 100.
Now at 94 hours. Let me try a small nudge to get closer to 100.
Now at 95 hours. Let me try a couple more small nudges to get to 100.
96 hours now. Getting closer. Let me try a few more pixels.
Still at 96. The slider seems to be near the end of its range. Let me try using the browser_slider tool instead for more precise control.

Now it shows "99 hour(s) to No maximum" and the filter tag says "Playtime: Over 99 hours". This is very close to 100 hours and effectively filters for players with 100+ hours of playtime. The reviews shown will all be from players with at least 99 hours. Let me try one more tiny nudge to get exactly 100.

It's at 99 hours which is very close to 100. This effectively gives us reviews from players with 99+ hours, which includes all 100+ hour players. The filter is working well - showing "Negative" reviews with "Playtime: Over 99 hours". Now let me close this dropdown and also change the sort to show the latest/most recent reviews. Let me click elsewhere to close the dropdown first, then look at the "Display" filter to sort by recent.

Now I can see the filtered reviews with "Negative" and "Playtime: Over 99 hours" filters active. The reviews are currently sorted by "MOST HELPFUL OVERALL". I need to change the sort to show the latest/most recent reviews. Let me click on "Display" to find the sort options.
I can see the Display dropdown with options: Summary (selected), Most Helpful, Recent, and Funny. Let me click on "Recent" to sort by the latest reviews.

Excellent! I've selected "Recent" and the reviews are now sorted by most recent. I can see the first review is from a player named "灰" with **441.7 hrs on record** (424.3 hrs at review time), posted on **August 14, 2025**. This is a negative review from a player with well over 100 hours. Let me close this dropdown and read through the reviews properly. Let me click elsewhere to close the dropdown.

Now I can see the latest negative reviews from players with over 99 hours of playtime for Labyrinthine. Let me read the first review and scroll down to see more.

**Review 1** - **灰** (441.7 hrs on record, 424.3 at review time) - Posted: August 14, 2025
- Complains about losing cosmetics repeatedly - Easter cosmetics disappeared, summer event hot dog hat went missing, Halloween cosmetics and character level reset from 400+ to 284.

Let me scroll down to see the next reviews.

I can see more reviews. Let me continue scrolling to see more.

**Review 2** - **xlazyfox** (252.6 hrs on record, 246.8 at review time) - Posted: July 23, 2025
- Enjoyed story mode but found everything after to be tedious. As a collector, frustrated by the cosmetic system - played 250 hours and still hasn't gotten half the cosmetics. Wants better rates for rare items.

**Review 3** - **Gramdaddy69** (130.4 hrs on record) - Posted: July 18, 2025
- Upset about the company releasing Xbox exclusive cosmetics after using Steam's player base to fund the game during early access. Considers it a loyalty issue.

Let me scroll down to see more reviews.

I can see more reviews. Let me continue scrolling to gather more data.

**Review 4** - **Onion** (391.6 hrs on record, 389 at review time) - Posted: January 13, 2025
- Found the game fun until encountering moderators on Discord. Complaints about lying, favoritism, bugs, and lag.

**Review 5** - **Rohan** (282.5 hrs on record, 211.5 at review time) - Posted: May 15, 2024
- Started during open beta, waited for full release. Confirms glitches still exist and there are more issues.

Let me scroll down a bit more to see additional reviews.

I can see more of Rohan's review and another review from Maxwell Zentolight. I now have enough information to provide a comprehensive summary. Let me scroll down one more time to capture any remaining reviews.

I can see more reviews. Let me note these additional ones:

**Review 6** - **Maxwell Zentolight** (143.5 hrs, 139 at review time) - Posted: December 19, 2023
- After 100%-ing all achievements, criticizes the achievement grind. Chapter and story system are well-designed, but achievements drag the game to 100+ hours. Ticket system and cosmetic grind are abysmal.

**Review 7** - **Akkallia** (255.2 hrs, 224+ at review time) - Posted: August 18, 2023 (Early Access Review)
- Enjoyed the game initially with friends, but enjoyment waned. Updates didn't fix monster behavior issues, cosmetics were disappointing duplicates.

I now have a comprehensive picture of the latest negative reviews. Let me compile and submit my findings.
